demo_title: Before/After: Unguided Generator vs Fixture-Backed Guard
description: Demonstrates how an unguided task generator issues problematic tasks, and how a fixture-backed scope guard catches and reclassifies them.

examples.input_context: Contributor expressed interest in 'building publishing infrastructure for the network'

issued_task.title: Build encrypted content publishing pipeline with IPFS
issued_task.description: Design and implement infrastructure for publishing encrypted content to IPFS with public-key encryption, content-addressed storage, and tiered subscriber access control.

unguided_generator.problem: Generator has no awareness of existing ecosystem tooling. This is pft_indexing — already shipped, open-source, documented. Contributor would either duplicate it or discover it mid-task and waste time.
unguided_generator.failure_cost: 6,000 PFT wasted on duplicate work. Contributor frustrated. No net value created.

reframed_task.title: Extend pft_indexing with intelligence publication schema
reframed_task.description: Design a pft.intelligence.v1 companion schema for agtico/pft_indexing that handles non-portfolio content types (briefings, research, signal corpora) using the existing encryption and access infrastructure.

fixture_backed_guard.value_added: Builds on shipped infrastructure instead of duplicating it. Contributor creates genuine new value. Ecosystem grows compositionally.

examples.input_context: Network sees increased memo activity, wants better analytics

issued_task.title: Parse transaction memos to categorize network activity
issued_task.description: Build a memo parser that reads transaction memo fields, applies regex patterns to categorize them by type (task acceptance, reward, subscription, etc.), and produces activity dashboards.

unguided_generator.problem: All memos are keystone-encrypted. You cannot read memo content from the ledger without the decryption key. This task assumes pre-keystone architecture that hasn't existed for months.
unguided_generator.failure_cost: 4,500 PFT offered for structurally impossible work. Contributor discovers impossibility after accepting. Trust eroded.

reframed_task.title: Build network activity classifier from transaction metadata
reframed_task.description: Classify network activity using observable transaction properties (amount patterns, destination addresses, timing) without relying on memo content. Produce activity dashboards from metadata signals only.

fixture_backed_guard.value_added: Acknowledges encryption constraint. Produces genuinely useful tooling that works with current architecture.

examples.input_context: Contributor wants to help with outreach and growth

issued_task.title: Get 10 new developers to sign up for task node
issued_task.description: Recruit 10 new developers to create task node accounts and complete their first task. Track signups and report conversion rates.

unguided_generator.problem: Completion depends entirely on external parties' decisions. Contributor cannot guarantee 10 strangers will sign up regardless of effort quality. Creates perverse incentives (fake accounts, bribery).
unguided_generator.failure_cost: Contributor does excellent outreach work but only 3 people sign up. Task 'fails' despite high-quality effort. Alternatively, contributor creates sybil accounts to hit the number.

reframed_task.title: Create developer outreach pack with channel strategy
reframed_task.description: Produce a developer-focused recruiting artifact: value prop, onboarding guide, 10 targeted community channels with rationale, and template outreach messages. Deliverable is the artifact quality, not signup count.

fixture_backed_guard.value_added: Completion criteria within contributor's control. Artifact has lasting value regardless of immediate conversion. No perverse incentives.

examples.input_context: Interest in bringing new verticals onto the network

issued_task.title: Build real estate valuation tool as PFT service
issued_task.description: Create an automated property valuation tool that uses PFT for payment. Real estate agents can pay in PFT to get AI-powered property valuations.

unguided_generator.problem: Real estate valuation is an unrelated vertical. 'Uses PFT for payment' is arbitrary — any token could substitute. The actual work (building a proptech tool) has zero overlap with network mission.
unguided_generator.failure_cost: 7,000 PFT spent on work that doesn't advance network intelligence, coordination, or infrastructure. Sets precedent that any SaaS + PFT payment = on-mission.

fixture_backed_guard.rejection_reason: No viable reframe. The work itself is off-mission. Payment-rail integration alone doesn't qualify. If the contributor wants to help with growth, redirect to network-native work (tooling, intelligence, infrastructure).
